</h2>

<p></p><p><strong>No matter what your art form is, you'll need to get better at photography. Photography is key to storytelling online. But if you REALLY want to catch people's eye, start incorporating the random people you meet in the stories you tell also. The results will be exponential, </strong></p><p><em>Here are 99 Photography Tips (for non-photographers) to get you started...</em></p><p>PHOTOGRAPHY TIP #1. Take 1,000 pictures a day. With intent. Intent means that you have a goal before you push the shutter. When the results start to match the intent, raise the bar on your intent. Repeat this until you die. At some point along the way someone may ask to publish one of your photos. It may even happen twice.</p><p>PHOTOGRAPHY TIP #2: Photography is showing what hides beyond the surface of what the eye usually sees.</p><p>PHOTOGRAPHY TIP #3: Once you have a connection with your model, your shot-list doesn't matter.</p><p>PHOTOGRAPHY TIP #4: There is no cure for cookie-cutter.</p><p>PHOTOGRAPHY TIP #5: Don't go in with expectations. But if the moment arises, seize it.</p><p>PHOTOGRAPHY TIP #6: Embrace being the third-wheel. It's opportunity in disguise.</p><p>PHOTOGRAPHY TIP #7: No one's in your room at 4 a.m. to do something normal.</p><p>PHOTOGRAPHY TIP #8: Pay attention to coincidences. They are synchronicity in disguise.</p><p>PHOTOGRAPHY TIP #9: Yes, you may be the painter... But always remember that it's the model who brings the palette.</p><p>PHOTOGRAPHY TIP #10: Ignore celebrities. Create new ones.</p><p>PHOTOGRAPHY TIP# 11: Sometimes photographers are like therapists. And sometimes it’s the model. </p><p>PHOTOGRAPHY TIP #12: Don't "steal" photos. Ask for permission. True, you may miss the moment. But it gives you access to new moments you could never have otherwise.</p><p>PHOTOGRAPHY TIP #13 It’s ok to use the same directions you used “last time”. Every model will give you a different interpretation of the same pose.</p><p>PHOTOGRAPHY TIP #14: Don’t plan your photoshoots. The amazing photographer that’s hiding inside of you only comes out to play when you improvise.</p><p>PHOTOGRAPHY TIP #15: If you can't find someone's wild side. Let their wild side find you.</p><p>PHOTOGRAPHY TIP #16: Everybody has a wild side. It’s up to you to find it.</p><p>PHOTOGRAPHY TIP #17: When you meet sisters. Never shoot one. Always shoot both.</p><p>PHOTOGRAPHY TIP #18: Always try to find out your model's hopes and dreams. Just talking about it will put a glimmer in her eye. And a glimmer is always photogenic.</p><p>PHOTOGRAPHY TIP #19: Bad photoshoots may happen, but they still make for good stories. There's no such thing as a bad photoshoot if it gives you a good story.</p><p>PHOTOGRAPHY TIP #20: When you shoot friends together, they will want to huddle like it's “vacation photos”. Encourage them to be individuals instead.</p><p>PHOTOGRAPHY TIP #21: When you do a group shot, people will tend to stand in a line-up. Use layering instead.</p><p>PHOTOGRAPHY TIP #29: Learn to love dirty tiles just as much as you love marbled floors.</p><p>PHOTOGRAPHY TIP #30: Reinvention should look a lot like invention... or you're doing it wrong.</p><p><span style="color: rgb(78, 78, 78);">PHOTOGRAPHY TIP #31: </span>Make it look like “anybody can do that”.</p><p>PHOTOGRAPHY TIP #35: When you’re a pioneer, half the world thinks you’re a genius, the other half thinks you’re shit. They’re both right until you have a body of work that proves one of them wrong.</p><p>PHOTOGRAPHY TIP #40: The best way to get your model out of their head is to have them participate in creating a moment.</p><p>PHOTOGRAPHY TIP #55: Don't photograph the first person you see. Photograph the second.</p><p>PHOTOGRAPHY TIP #62: Sometimes the person you want to shoot has even better friends.</p><p>PHOTOGRAPHY TIP #63: The only thing more unhealthy than break-up sex is a break-up photo shoot.</p><p>PHOTOGRAPHY TIP #64: If she starts texting her ex... The photo shoot is over.</p><p>PHOTOGRAPHY TIP #65: Sometimes, a hotel room can be more inspiring than a whole city.</p><p>PHOTOGRAPHY TIP #67: The best pictures happen when you try to have your cake and shoot it too. </p><p>PHOTOGRAPHY TIP #68: There is less and less oxygen as you climb to the top.</p><p>PHOTOGRAPHY TIP #69: You're nobody until you're an item on someone's bucket list.</p><p>PHOTOGRAPHY TIP #70: A great outfit can inspire even the smallest minds into submission. </p><p>PHOTOGRAPHY TIP #71: What people call boring nights are really just missed connections.</p><p>PHOTOGRAPHY TIP #72: If you want to make the world look a little more beautiful. There’s only one option: Fall in love with where you are in that moment.</p><p>PHOTOGRAPHY TIP #73: Don't let your relationship deteriorate to the point where a stranger can take a better picture of your significant other than you.</p><p>PHOTOGRAPHY TIP #74: Embrace your awkward side. It'll attract a whole new demographic.</p><p>PHOTOGRAPHY TIP #75: Be shy. Be courageous. Be tender. Be bold. They’re all photographable.</p><p>PHOTOGRAPHY TIP #76: The exciting thing about people isn’t who they are... It’s who they can become. Especially with Photoshop.</p><p>PHOTOGRAPHY TIP #77: Sometimes, connecting with someone starts by simply letting them in.</p><p>PHOTOGRAPHY TIP #78: Highlighter under the eyebrows is magical.</p><p><span style="color: rgb(78, 78, 78);">PHOTOGRAPHY TIP #79: </span>When you go to an art show, always remember that the art isn’t only on the walls.</p><p>PHOTOGRAPHY TIP #84: Once you get good at photography, people will start to say things like, “You only photograph attractive people.” But you will secretly know they’re wrong. A photographer can only photograph what they see. You become a GOOD photographer when you can turn what you see into a picture that matches. Your photos will become a reflection of the excitement you feel for the person or situation in front of you. If you cherish beauty, suddenly everyone will look “beautiful”. Bad photographers are the ones who try to use shortcuts like "shooting models" hoping to compensate for their lack of skills. But all they end up with is a lousy photo of a good-looking person. Why do you think even the hottest people are picky about who shoots them? Because they understand that physical attractiveness won’t ever make a photo great. To get better photographs start dedicating yourself to photographing people’s energy instead.</p><p></p>
</div>
